verbs.notes: Present indicative forms are enclitic except εἰμί (1st sg). No aorist, perfect, or passive forms. Future is suppletive from ἔσομαι.
Stem-final π produces consonant cluster changes at junctures: γράφ + σ = γράψ (future/aorist), γράφ + κ = γράφ (perfect active).
Present and imperfect exist in middle/passive forms only. Perfect active (γέγονα) uses the shorter γεν- stem. 6th principal part (ἐγενήθην) is rare in classical Attic.

Epsilon-contract verb: stem ποιε- contracts with endings in the present/imperfect system (ε+ε→ει, ε+ο→ου, ε+long vowel/diphthong→long vowel/diphthong absorbs ε). Other tenses lengthen ε to η and conjugate like a regular verb.
